title: RNA Extraction with Trizol for Tissues
author: Maysa Silva
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.stmeek6

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Trizol to...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Trizol to amounts of 50 to 100 mg and homogenize with the homogenizer. Pass to a tube. In this step, you can freeze this sample for up to 6 months in the -80C freezer or continue the protocol.

steps.title: Incubate for 5 minutes to permit...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 5 minutes to permit complete dissociation of the nucleoproteins complex.

steps.title: Add 200 μl L of chloroform...
steps.instruction: Add 200 μl L of chloroform per 1 mL of TRIzol and incubate for 2 to 3 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge the sample for 15 minutes...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the sample for 15 minutes at 12,000 × g at 4°C.

steps.title: The mixture separates into a lower...
steps.instruction: The mixture separates into a lower red phenol-chloroform, and interphase, and a colorless upper aqueous phase. Transfer the aqueous (incolor) phase containing the RNA to a new tube by angling the tube at 45° and pipetting the solution out.

steps.title: Add 500 μl of isopropanol per 1 mL of TRIzol
steps.instruction: Add 500 μl of isopropanol per 1 mL of TRIzol. Incubate for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 12
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 10 minutes at 12.000 × g at 4°C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant with a micropipetto...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant with a micropipetto and resuspend the pellet in 1 mL of 75% ethanol per 1 mL of TRIzol.

steps.title: Vortex the sample briefly and centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Vortex the sample briefly and centrifuge for 5 minutes at 7.500 × g at 4°C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant with a micropipettor
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant with a micropipettor. Vacuum or air dry the RNA pellet for 5–10 minutes.

steps.title: Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 µL of RNase-free water, 0
steps.instruction: Resuspend the pellet in 20–50 µL of RNase-free water, 0.1 mM EDTA, or 0.5% SDS solution by pipetting up and down. Note: Do not dissolve the RNA in 0.5% SDS if the RNA is to be used in subsequent enzymatic reactions.

steps.title: Incubate in a water bath or...
steps.instruction: Incubate in a water bath or heat block set at 55–60°C for 10–15 minutes. Then store in -80°C freezer.
